a23: Example key with value ';Amy23;' used for tests throughout codebase.
a23_dash: Example key with value '-Amy23-' used for tests throughout codebase.
a23_slash: Example key with value '/Amy23/' used for tests throughout codebase.
active_requisite: Represents the expected boolean of the PlanUnit with address reason_context. If None, no check. If False expected Active bool of reason_context PlanUnit is false. If True expected contest_plan.active is True, Reason seed
addin: float that describes addition to parent range calculations, Plan seed
allowed_crud: Each idea config dimen has an allowed_crud that describes whether the data may be updated/deleted/inserted more than one.
Anaday: First Day of 5 day week of 'Five' Epoch
April: Example key with value 'April' used for tests throughout codebase.
assert: Used so often in Python that it cannot be given any kegology meaning.
atom: Smallest possible unit of a PersonUnit
attributes: General term used to describe things about another thing
August: Example key with value 'August' used for tests throughout codebase.
awardheirs: Every PlanUnit has AwardHeirs that represent who is awarded give_fund and take_fund. AwardHeirs have every AwardUnit group_title for every PlanUnit Ancestor back to root, Plan conpute
awardlines: Every PlanUnit has AwardLines that represent who is awarded give_fund and take_fund. AwardLines have every AwardUnit group_title for every PlanUnit Descedant starting at the childless PlanUnit, Plan conpute
awardunits: Every PlanUnit has AwardUnits that represent who is awarded give_fund and take_fund, Plan seed
Baileyday: Second Day of 5 day week of 'Five' Epoch
begin: float that describes the begin of a numerical range if it exists, Plan seed
bele_src: 5 character abbreviation of belve_src. stage_type_order=1 Belief Source files. Idea formats without SparkNum.
beli_dst: 5 character abbreviation of belve_dst. stage_type_order=14 Belief Destination files. Idea formats without SparkNum.
belief: A collection of ideas (currently only in idea format) with a spark_face but no spark_num.
belve_dst: stage_type_order=14 Belief Destination files. Idea formats without SparkNum.
belve_src: stage_type_order=1 Belief Source files. Idea formats without SparkNum.
blue: Example key with value 'blue' used for tests throughout codebase.
bob: Example key with value 'Bob' used for tests throughout codebase.
bowl: Example key with value 'bowl' used for tests throughout codebase.
br_agg: 5 character abbreviation of ideax_agg. stage_type_order=4 Idea Agg: The Idea Raw tables are aggregated to elimanate duplicates.
br_raw: 5 character abbreviation of ideax_raw. stage_type_order=3 Idea Raw: The importable Excel Sheets entering the database
br_vld: 5 character abbreviation of ideax_vld. stage_type_order=6 Idea Valid: The Idea Agg tables filtered to correct datatypes.
break: Used so often in Python that it cannot be given any kegology meaning.
BreakTerm: str first used in ch02. All str separator classes are BreakTerms. Meant to envoke the analogy of the necessity to break experience reality into things so they can be understood.
bud_contact_nets: ledger of bud outcome. dict[ContactName, FundNum]. FundNum can be negative.
bud_time: When a budget adds to the supply, bud arg, Nabuable arg
build_order: When building a world the order of dimen data implementation must be deterministic. build_order defines the order dimens are evaluated for a World.
c100_length: A C400Constant for building Epochs: 52,594,560 minutes in 100 year cycle
c400_core: 400 years without the extra leap day.
c400_core_length: A C400Constant for building Epochs: 210,378,240 minutes in 400 year cycle without last leap-day
c400_leap: 400 years with the extra leap day.
c400_leap_length: A C400Constant for building Epochs: 210,379,680 minutes in 400 year cycle
c400_number: Epoch config Integer that represents number of 400 year cycles. Defines the range of years in epoch c400_number=7 means 2800 years, Moment arg
calc_by_conpute: Describes when data is defined by conpute process. Not required seed data.
casa: Example key with value 'casa' used for tests throughout codebase.
celldepth: , used with Budget Cells, bud arg
ch00: Creates helper tools for OS and python data manipulation.
ch01: Defines how to allot a pool number to a weighted ledger.
ch02: Defines Contacts, Groups, Memberships, Awardees debt and cred.
ch03: Defines Workforce, LaborUnit. How tasks are assigned to labor.
ch04: Defines Term Classes: Knots, Labels, RopeTerms, FirstLabels
ch05: Defines ReasonUnits, FactUnits, and Facts decide if a Reason is Active
ch06: Defines PlanUnits with sub-plans, Awardees, Workforce, Reasons, Facts, etc.
ch07: Defines a PersonUnit as a PlanUnit and ContactUnits capable of producing an agenda.
ch08: Defines PersonAtoms: Irreducible units of change for a PersonUnit.
ch09: Defines lessons which are collections of PersonAtoms by FaceName
ch10: These tools describe how one PersonUnit listens to another.
ch11: Defines PersonBudHistory: PersonName, BudUnits (TimeNum, quota, celldepth)
ch12: Builds a simulation that describes how much credit a healer has earned.
ch13: Defines how time is handled by PersonUnits. Allows for arbitrary time structures.
ch14: Defines MomentUnits: common time tech, ledger, ContactNames, history of PersonUnits.
ch15: Defines NabuUnit tools that interpret numeric values into inside accounting.
ch16: Defines TranslateUnit tools that translate MomentUnits into inside language.
ch17: Defines how to build Ideas using Ideas. Moments and Persons are built by Ideas.
ch18: Defines the tools that use Idea Ideas to build MomentUnits.
ch19: Defines the tools that report from core world data.
ch20: Defines the analytics outcomes of completed MomentUnits.
ch21: Defines WorldDirs that admin MomentUnits input data and outcomes
ch22: Defines Lobby Tools for gifts, World Scenarios.
ch30: ETL app to create day punches from excel files.
Chiday: Third Day of 5 day week of 'Five' Epoch
class: Used so often in Python that it cannot be given any kegology meaning.
class_type: Used idenify an attribute's object class. Usually in config files.
clean: Example key with value 'clean' used for tests throughout codebase.
close: float that describes the close of a numerical range if it exists, Plan seed
column_ordinal: Describes which column in a table the attribute should have. The number '2' would be 3rd column because of starting at 0 index.
conpute: The process by which a PersonUnit creates all it's output metrics from file-stored data. Meant to invoke contemplation and computation. The PersonUnit contemplates what is important to them (their plans) and decides what they want to do. They compute their rules and create their agenda. If the PersonUnit plans are trusted with a currency quota they can be used as an Budget. Each Contact is assigned a FundNum. It may be negative.
contact_name: , paybook arg, Contact seed, Member seed
ContactName: str first used in ch02. Every ContactName object is NameTerm, must follow NameTerm format.
creg: Gregorian time does not have year zero. It was necessary to have year zero so the default epoch technology is Cregorian time, Gregorian time with year 0.
crud: Create, Read, Update, Delete. The main things that can be done in a database.
CRUD_command: str first used in ch08 that represents database CRUD commands
cuisine: Example key with value 'cuisine' used for tests throughout codebase.
Danceday: Fourth Day of 5 day week of 'Five' Epoch
dash: Example key with value '-' used for tests throughout codebase.
day: A fundamental structure of time for Keg2. Always assumed to be a 1440 minutes long.
day_length: A C400Constant for building Epochs: 1,440 minutes in day
day_punch: A report that summarizes the tasks of a person for a day.
dayly_duration_min: Required fields for creating Epoch dayly cases.
dayly_lower_min: Required fields for creating Epoch dayly cases.
days_lower_day: Required fields for creating Epoch every_xdays cases.
days_upper_day: Required fields for creating Epoch every_xdays cases.
December: Example key with value 'December' used for tests throughout codebase.
DELETE: CRUD_COMMAND to delete records for a table
delete_insert_update: Associated dimens: person_contact_membership, person_contactunit, person_plan_awardunit, person_plan_factunit, person_plan_reason_caseunit, person_plan_reasonunit, person_planunit' in 'Associated dimens: person_planunit, person_contactunit, person_plan_reasonunit, person_plan_factunit, person_plan_awardunit, person_contact_membership, person_plan_reason_caseunit
denom: int that describes denominator to parent range calculations, Plan seed
dirtyness: Example key with value 'dirtyness' used for tests throughout codebase.
dive: To go from living on land to swimming with fearlessness. Not used in codebase.
Eastday: Fifth Day of 5 day week of 'Five' Epoch
ep8: Example key with value ';ep8;' used for tests throughout codebase.
epoch_label: Epoch config: Name of epoch. Persons may have multiple epochs, they are differentiated by this LabelTerm, Moment arg
epoch_length: Total number of minutes in a Epoch. Calculated by number of minutes in C400_leap times Epoch's c400_number
EpochLabel: str first used in ch13. EpochLabel is required for every EpochUnit. It is a LabelTerm that must not contain the knot.
every_xdays: Required fields for creating Epoch every_xdays cases.
every_xweeks: Required fields for creating Epoch weekly cases.
exam_tier: Every Keyword has an exam_tier. The highter the exam_tier the more relatively important the keyword.
FaceName: str first used in ch09. The Face is the source of all outside data.
fact: Facts are very important concept. I tried never to use those 4 letters anywhere that didn't refer exactly to that concept.
fact_context: All facts have a foundational context, Fact seed.
fact_state: A fact can have a state different from its context, a different branch of the context where all other branches are not in fact, Fact seed.
FactNum: float first used in ch04. A numeric value that may converted to other Semantic Types by an external process driven by context.
factory: Not used in codebase. The factory method in programming is very important. However because I want 'fact' only to refer to the Keg concept I don't use the word 'factory', instead I use 'shop'.
factunits: PlanUnit holds all it's factunits in an attribute called factunits.
February: Example key with value 'February' used for tests throughout codebase.
filename: Should always include extension ala filebasename + '.' + extension
FirstLabel: str first used in ch04. The first LabelTerm in a RopeTerm. FirstLabel cannot contain knot. Not called RootLabel to avoid confusion.
five: An Epoch. A structure for time often used in the codebase. Starts about 5000 years ago, has 16 months, 15 of which are 25 days. Week is 5 days long.
Friday: Example key with value 'Friday' used for tests throughout codebase.
fund_cease: FundNum number at which funding ceases inside master funding range, Plan conpute
fund_give: , Contact conpute, Award conpute, Member conpute data
fund_grain: , Moment arg, Person seed, Plan conpute.
fund_onset: FundNum number at which funding onsets inside master funding range, Plan conpute
fund_take: , Contact conpute, Award conpute, Member conpute data
FundGrain: float first used in ch02. Smallest Unit of fund_num
FundNum: float first used in ch02. FundNum inherits from float class
global: Used so often in Python that it cannot be given any kegology meaning.
GrainNum: float first used in ch01. GrainNum represents the smallest fraction allowed
GroupMark: str first used in ch02. GroupMark(s) exist in TitleTerms to indicate its a group, no GroupMark indicates it is a ContactName.
GroupTitle: str first used in ch02. If contains SepartorTerms(s) it represents a group otherwise its a ContactName.
h_agg: 5 character abbreviation of heard_agg. stage_type_order=11 Populated by aggregated Heard Raw tables. Nabu number conversions take place here.
h_raw: 5 character abbreviation of heard_raw. stage_type_order=10 Populated by Sound Valid tables
h_vld: 5 character abbreviation of heard_vld. stage_type_order=12 Populated by aggregated and Nabu changed Heard Agg tables
HealerName: str first used in ch02. A NameTerm used to identify a Problem's Healer
heard_agg: stage_type_order=11 Populated by aggregated Heard Raw tables. Nabu number conversions take place here.
heard_raw: stage_type_order=10 Populated by Sound Valid tables
heard_vld: stage_type_order=12 Populated by aggregated and Nabu changed Heard Agg tables
hn_blu: Example key with value ';herenow_blu;' used for tests throughout codebase.
hn_red: Example key with value ';herenow_red;' used for tests throughout codebase.
hn_yel: Example key with value ';herenow_yel;' used for tests throughout codebase.
hours_config: Epoch config List of Lists that contain two elements: hour name and the day's minute when it ends. Last item list must contain hour name and 1440 because that is the last minute of the day, partial example: [['12am', 60], ['11pm', 1440]]
i_src_dir: Source Directory of Idea files. Often Belief files with spark_num added.
idea_src: 5 character abbreviation of ideaa_src. stage_type_order=2 Idea files. Idea formats with SparkNum.
ideaa_src: stage_type_order=2 Idea files. Idea formats with SparkNum.
ideax_agg: stage_type_order=4 Idea Agg: The Idea Raw tables are aggregated to elimanate duplicates.
ideax_raw: stage_type_order=3 Idea Raw: The importable Excel Sheets entering the database
ideax_vld: stage_type_order=6 Idea Valid: The Idea Agg tables filtered to correct datatypes.
ii: Idea Format Identifier. Every idea_type format starts with 'ii' and then 5 numbers.
import: Used so often in Python that it cannot be given any kegology meaning.
infinity: As described in Totality and Infinity. Not used in codebase.
init_chapter: Every keyword must have an init_chapter that describes the first chapter the keyword is allowed to appear in.
INSERT: CRUD_COMMAND to delete records for a table
insert_one_time: Associated dimens: moment_epoch_hour, moment_epoch_month, moment_epoch_weekday, momentunit, nabu_timenum, translate_label, translate_name, translate_rope, translate_title
inx_knot: , TRANSLATE_LABEL arg, TRANSLATE_NAME arg, TRANSLATE_ROPE arg, TRANSLATE_TITLE arg
January: Example key with value 'January' used for tests throughout codebase.
jkeys: The term 'keys' was too generic. 'jkeys' is less generic.
July: Example key with value 'July' used for tests throughout codebase.
jump: To use keg requires a human to jump and try. Not easy. Not used in codebase.
June: Example key with value 'June' used for tests throughout codebase.
Keyword: Reserved word that is supposed to have one meaning.
keyword: Reserved word that is supposed to have one meaning.
knot: , Moment arg, Person seed, Plan seed, Reason seed, Case seed, Fact seed, Award seed, Workforce seed, Healer seed
KnotTerm: str first used in ch04. A string to used as a delimiter in RopeTerms.
LabelTerm: str first used in ch04. A string representation of a tree node. Nodes cannot contain RopeTerm knot
LobbyID: str first used in ch22. str used to name LobbyUnit
lynx: 5 character abbreviation of lynxx_mst. stage_type_order=13 File buildout of Moments and PersonUnits. Word used to describe the end state of the core ETL pipeline. All core data should be in the database. Files may still be accessible. KPIs should all be sourced from it.
lynxx_mst: stage_type_order=13 File buildout of Moments and PersonUnits
mana_grain: , Moment arg, used with Budget Cells, Person seed.
ManaGrain: float first used in ch04. Smallest Unit of Mana.
ManaNum: float first used in ch12. ManaNum inherits from float class
March: Example key with value 'March' used for tests throughout codebase.
match: Used so often in Python that it cannot be given any kegology meaning.
May: Example key with value 'May' used for tests throughout codebase.
moment_ote1_agg: Create Database Table that holds all spark_num to bud_time pairs. Include moment_rope and person_name
moment_rope: , Moment arg, offi_time arg, paybook arg, mmtweek arg, mmtmont arg, mmthour arg, bud arg, Person seed, Plan seed, Reason seed, Case seed, Fact seed, Award seed, Workforce seed, Healer seed, Contact seed, Member seed, nabu arg.
moment_static: Used to identify if the data can change or must be identify when the Moment of instantization.
MomentRope: str first used in ch09. The RopeTerm for a Moment. Must contain knots.
Monday: Example key with value 'Monday' used for tests throughout codebase.
monthday_index: Epoch config: Changes MonthDay displayed day number. Months' day numbers are assumed to start with 0. The first day of the month is #0. The 3rd day of the month would be Day #2 of month. Most audiences prefer The first day of the month to be 1 even though this makes divisor difficult. If monthday_index=1 then the 3rd day of the month is #3, Moment arg
monthly_duration_days: Required fields for creating Epoch monthly cases.
monthly_monthday_lower: Required fields for creating Epoch monthly cases.
months_config: Epoch config: List of lists that contain two elements: month name and the year's day when it ends. Last item list must contain year's day equal 365 because it is the last day of the year. [['March', 31], ['February', 365]]
mop: Example key with value 'mop' used for tests throughout codebase.
morph: bool that describes how to change parent range in calculations, Plan seed
NameTerm: str first used in ch02. All Name string classes should inherit from this class
November: Example key with value 'November' used for tests throughout codebase.
numor: int that describes numerator to parent range calculations, Plan seed
October: Example key with value 'October' used for tests throughout codebase.
otx_knot: , TRANSLATE_LABEL arg, TRANSLATE_NAME arg, TRANSLATE_ROPE arg, TRANSLATE_TITLE arg
parent_rope: A parent_rope of rope is created by removing the last label. For PlanUnits the parent_rope is the key for their parent PlanUnit.
parent_solo: The parent PlanUnit's LaborUnit's solo value for that labor_title
person_contact_membership: A person dimen representing Membership data. Keys: person_name, contact_name, group_title. person seed.
person_contactunit: A person dimen representing ParterUnit data. Keys: person_name, contact_name. person seed.
person_groupunit: A person dimen representing GroupUnit data. Keys: person_name, group_title. person conpute.
person_name: , paybook arg, bud arg, Person seed, Plan seed, Reason seed, Case seed, Fact seed, Award seed, Workforce seed, Healer seed, Contact seed, Member seed.
person_plan_awardunit: A person dimen representing AwardUnit data. Keys: person_name, plan_rope, awardee_title. person seed.
person_plan_factunit: A person dimen representing FactUnit data. Keys: person_name, plan_rope, fact_context. person seed.
person_plan_healerunit: A person dimen representing HealerUnit data. Keys: person_name, plan_rope, healer_name. person seed.
person_plan_laborunit: A person dimen representing HealerUnit data. Keys: person_name, plan_rope, labor_title. person seed.
person_plan_reason_caseunit: A person dimen representing CaseUnit data. Keys: person_name, plan_rope, reason_context, reason_state. person seed.
person_plan_reasonunit: A person dimen representing ReasonUnit data. Keys: person_name, plan_rope, reason_context. person seed.
person_planunit: A person dimen representing PlanUnit data. Keys: person_name, plan_rope. person seed.
PersonName: str first used in ch07. The LabelTerm used to identify a PersonUnit. Must be a LabelTerm/NameTerm because when identifying if a PlanUnit is an active pledge the PersonName will be compared against ContactNames. If they match the pledge will be active.
personunit: A person dimen representing PersonUnit data. Keys: person_name. person seed.
plan_label: Every planunit has a plan_label. Every other sibling planunit must have a different plan label.
plan_rope: , Plan seed, Reason seed, Case seed, Fact seed, Award seed, Workforce seed, Healer seed.
plan_task: A plan_task planUnit can change a FactUnit and change Active Plan without plan_task, Plan conpute
plan_uid: A uniqueidentifier for a planunit. Currently at the person level. Perhaps at a global level in the future.
planroot: Every PersonUnit has one planunit called the planroot. It can have child planunits and those can have child planunits, etc.
PoolNum: float first used in ch01. PoolNum represents any possible subset of the sum of numbers in the pool.
quota: The importance of a BudUnit. The currency given to the BudUnit to be distributed to Contacts. The magnitude number will always be less then quote because its the sum of how much of the quota is distributed, bud arg, used with Budget Cells
range_duration: Required fields for creating Epoch ranged cases.
range_inheritors:
# range_inheritors key: plan_rope for any Plan that inherits from a range_plan
    # range_inheritors value: plan_rope for the range_plan
    # During the conpute process range_inheritors defines when range_inheritors factheirs need to be created
range_lower_min: Required fields for creating Epoch ranged cases.
rational: If a PersonUnit's agenda is deterministic (always the same after conpute) then value is True, Person conpute
ReasonNum: float first used in ch04. A numeric value that may converted to other Semantic Types by an external process driven by context.
red: Example key with value 'red' used for tests throughout codebase.
RespectGrain: float first used in ch02. Smallest Unit of score (RespectNum) ala 'the slightest bit of respect!'
RespectNum: float first used in ch02. RespectNum inherits from float class
RopeTerm: str first used in ch04. A string representation of a tree path. LabelTerms are seperated by knots.
run: Example key with value ';runners' used for tests throughout codebase.
run_dash: Example key with value '-runners' used for tests throughout codebase.
s_agg: 5 character abbreviation of sound_agg. stage_type_order=8 Populated by aggregated Sound Raw dimen data (elimanate duplicates). Translate Core data is validated then used to translate non-Translate dimen data
s_raw: 5 character abbreviation of sound_raw. stage_type_order=7 The Idea Valid data is seperated by column into dimen data such as person_planunit and moment_budunit
s_vld: 5 character abbreviation of sound_vld. stage_type_order=9 The Translated Sound Agg data is aggregated to elimanate duplicates into Sound Valid
Saturday: Example key with value 'Saturday' used for tests throughout codebase.
scrub: Example key with value 'scrub' used for tests throughout codebase.
self: Used so often in Python that it cannot be given any kegology meaning.
semantic_type: A datatype that is fundamentally int, str, or float
September: Example key with value 'September' used for tests throughout codebase.
sheet: Object that represents a spreadsheet. It has attributes like filename, table_name, and data.
shop: The factory method in programming is very important. However because I want 'fact' only to refer to the Keg concept I don't use the word 'factory', instead I use 'shop'.
slash: Example key with value '/' used for tests throughout codebase.
solo: I can't figure out what this is for. Where in the codebase is it necessary for a LaborUnit to know if it the only LaborUnit in a WorkforceUnit, Workforce seed.
sound_agg: stage_type_order=8 Populated by aggregated Sound Raw dimen data (elimanate duplicates). Translate Core data is validated then used to translate non-Translate dimen data
sound_raw: stage_type_order=7 The Idea Valid data is seperated by column into dimen data such as person_planunit and moment_budunit
sound_vld: stage_type_order=9 The Translated Sound Agg data is aggregated to elimanate duplicates into Sound Valid
spark_face: Every spark has a spark_face that identifies which Face it belongs to. The Face is the source of all outside data. In theory a spark_face can be any data type. In practice it is currently a string data type.
spark_num: Every spark has a spark_num that identifies its order of ingestion. Sparks are orderd but not necessarily sequential. Spark_num is an int. , used with Budget Cells
SparkInt: int first used in ch11. Each Spark is a int that describes the order of data ingestion
square: Example key with value 'square' used for tests throughout codebase.
sue: Example key with value 'Sue' used for tests throughout codebase.
Sunday: Example key with value 'Sunday' used for tests throughout codebase.
sweep: Example key with value 'sweep' used for tests throughout codebase.
swim: To live with faith. To be slave to many masters instead of a slave to one. To live in many moments. Not used in codebase.
Thursday: Example key with value 'Thursday' used for tests throughout codebase.
TimeNum: int first used in ch11. An Integar that can represent a instant on the TimeNumLine
TitleTerm: str first used in ch02. If a TitleTerm contains SepartorTerms(s) it represents a group otherwise its a single member group of an ContactName.
today: Refences the computer's current date, used to create a day_punch.
totality: As described in Totality and Infinity. Not used in codebase.
translate_label: 'translate_label' is a translation dimen where otx_label is translated by inx_knot, inx_label, otx_knot, unknown_str
translate_name: 'translate_name' is a translation dimen where otx_name is translated by inx_knot, inx_name, otx_knot, unknown_str
translate_rope: 'translate_rope' is a translation dimen where otx_rope is translated by inx_knot, inx_rope, otx_knot, unknown_str
translate_title: 'translate_title' is a translation dimen where otx_title is translated by inx_knot, inx_title, otx_knot, unknown_str
Tuesday: Example key with value 'Tuesday' used for tests throughout codebase.
unknown_str: , TRANSLATE_LABEL arg, TRANSLATE_NAME arg, TRANSLATE_ROPE arg, TRANSLATE_TITLE arg
UPDATE: CRUD_COMMAND to update records for a table. 'Associated dimens: personunit' in 'CRUD_COMMAND to update records for a table'
wed: Example key with value 'Wed' used for tests throughout codebase.
Wednesday: Example key with value 'Wednesday' used for tests throughout codebase.
week: A fundamental structure of time for Keg2. Always assumed to be a days long. Never partial days.
weekdays_config: Epoch config, List of Weekday names. When the Epoch begins the first Weekday in the list is the first day of the Epoch. ['Wednesday', ...'Tuesday'],
weekly_duration_min: Required fields for creating Epoch weekly cases.
weekly_lower_min: Required fields for creating Epoch weekly cases.
weeks_lower_week: Required fields for creating Epoch weekly cases.
weeks_upper_week: Required fields for creating Epoch weekly cases.
WeightNum: float first used in ch01. WeightNum represents the unnormalized value of a ledger key. The sum of all WeightNum values in a ledger is not defined.
wk: Example key with value 'wk' used for tests throughout codebase.
WorldName: str first used in ch21. Name of WorldDir
xio: Example key with value 'Xio' used for tests throughout codebase.
yao: Example key with value 'Yao' used for tests throughout codebase.
year: A fundamental structure of time for Keg2. Always 365 days. The leap day is a special other thing in Keg2.
year_length: A C400Constant for building Epochs: 525,600 minutes in year without leap-day
year_monthday_duration_days: Required fields for creating Epoch year_monthday cases.
year_monthday_lower: Required fields for creating Epoch year_monthday cases.
yield: Used so often in Python that it cannot be given any kegology meaning.
yr1_jan1_offset: Epoch config: Number of minutes the calendar is different from March 1, year 1 where Gregorian calendar is projected to year 1. Example 440640 is almost 10 months, Moment arg
yr4_core: A fundamental structure of time for Keg2. Includes 4 years of 365 days, plan_label for Epoch
yr4_core_length: A C400Constant for building Epochs: 2,102,400 minutes in 4 year cycle without leap-day
yr4_leap: A fundamental structure of time for Keg2. Includes 4 years of 365 days (yr4_core) and a leap day. The leap day is the last day.
yr4_leap_length: A C400Constant for building Epochs: 2,103,840 minutes in 4 year cycle
zia: Example key with value 'Zia' used for tests throughout codebase.
